user_query:
In the spirit of calm precision, please coordinate a 90-minute "Mindful Detailing Session" by finding a time that all named participants can attend and preparing the artifacts I need. Use only the files in this workspace, interpret all dates/times in Europe/Rome, and write outputs under out/.

Inputs to use:
- input/owner_availability.csv
- input/attendees_availability.csv
- input/preferences.json
- input/cars.json
- docs/agenda_template.md
- notes/invite_template.md
- scheduler/config.yaml
- notes/retreat_calendar.md

Requirements:
1) Compute all candidate meeting slots of exactly duration_minutes from preferences.json that fit the intersection of the Zen Master’s availability (owner_availability.csv) and every attendee listed in preferences.json (cross-reference their rows in attendees_availability.csv). A candidate slot must be a contiguous start–end interval fully within each participant’s availability for the same date. For each maximal overlap window on a date, use the earliest feasible start time as a single candidate (do not generate rolling or overlapping starts). Label each candidate as window_label = "preferred_window" if its start time is within preferences.preferred_window.start–end (inclusive of start, exclusive of end), otherwise "off_window".
2) Score candidates: score = morning_weight (from preferences.json) if window_label = "preferred_window", else 0. Rank by score descending, then by date ascending, then by start time ascending. Export all candidates and their scores to out/slots_ranked.csv with columns exactly: date (YYYY-MM-DD), start (HH:MM), end (HH:MM), window_label, score.
3) Select the top-ranked slot as the final booking. Choose the focus vehicle as the car with the highest priority_score in input/cars.json (break ties by earliest detailing_due_date, then by name alphabetically). Save the final choice to out/final_selection.json with fields exactly: title, date, start, end, timezone, location, attendees (array), car, score.
4) Rewrite the agenda and invitation from the templates:
   - Fill docs/agenda_template.md placeholders {{TITLE}}, {{DATE}}, {{START}}, {{END}}, {{TIMEZONE}}, {{LOCATION}}, {{ATTENDEES}}, {{CAR}} and write the result to out/agenda.md.
   - Fill notes/invite_template.md placeholders {{TITLE}}, {{DATE}}, {{START}}, {{END}}, {{TIMEZONE}}, {{LOCATION}}, {{ATTENDEES}}, {{DURATION}}, {{CAR}} and write the result to out/invite.md.
   Use preferences.json for meeting_title, duration_minutes, timezone, location, and attendees. Use the chosen slot and car for date/time/car fields. Keep template text unchanged apart from placeholder substitution.
5) Modify scheduler/config.yaml in place: set default_duration_minutes to the chosen duration_minutes and append the booked event under events as a new item with keys: title, date, start, end, timezone, location, attendees, car. Preserve existing fields and formatting where reasonable.
6) Edit notes/retreat_calendar.md in place: under the "## Scheduled" section, append one line exactly in this pattern (single line):
   - YYYY-MM-DD START–END (Europe/Rome) - Mindful Detailing Session @ Dojo Garage [Car: <CAR>] — Attendees: Zen Master, Aiko, Luca
   Do not alter other lines.

Deliverables to produce:
- out/slots_ranked.csv (all candidates with scores, sorted as specified)
- out/final_selection.json (the booked slot and chosen car)
- out/agenda.md (filled agenda)
- out/invite.md (filled invitation)
- Modified scheduler/config.yaml (updated duration and appended event)
- Modified notes/retreat_calendar.md (one new Scheduled line as specified)

Everything must be derived from the provided inputs without using the Internet. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
